title.tr: Page File / Sanal Bellek Boyutlandirma (Microsoft Learn)
title.en: Page File / Virtual Memory Sizing (Microsoft Learn)

risk_note.tr: Adimlar Microsoft Learn 'How to determine the appropriate page file size for 64-bit versions of Windows' belgesinden alinmistir. Microsoft varsayilani 'system-managed' (dinamik) - cogu kullanici icin ideal. Sabit boyut yalniz spesifik nedenlerle (SSD parcalanmayi onleme, dedicated dump partition) tercih edilir.
risk_note.en: Steps sourced from Microsoft Learn 'How to determine the appropriate page file size for 64-bit versions of Windows'. Microsoft default is 'system-managed' (dynamic) - ideal for most users. Fixed size only for specific reasons (avoid SSD fragmentation, dedicated dump partition).

body.tr: Microsoft alintisi: 'By default, page files are system-managed. This system management means that the page files increase and decrease based on many factors, such as the amount of physical memory installed, the process of accommodating the system commit charge, and the process of accommodating a system crash dump.' Yani Windows kendi optimal boyutu hesaplar - el ile karismadan once nedenini netlestirin.
body.en: Microsoft quote: 'By default, page files are system-managed. This system management means that the page files increase and decrease based on many factors, such as the amount of physical memory installed, the process of accommodating the system commit charge, and the process of accommodating a system crash dump.' Windows auto-calculates optimal - clarify your reason before manual override.

body.tr: Microsoft 'RAM x 1.5' gibi kural-i-bin onermez. Bunun yerine 'system commit charge' olcumu: Task Manager > Performance > Memory > Committed degeri. Veya performance counter '\Memory\Committed Bytes' ve '\Memory\Commit Limit'. Microsoft alintisi: 'Use this counter to determine whether the pagefile is an appropriate size. Depending on the volatility of your workload, you probably want the pagefile large enough so that it is no more than 50-75 percent used.'
body.en: Microsoft does NOT recommend a 'RAM x 1.5' rule of thumb. Instead, measure 'system commit charge': Task Manager > Performance > Memory > Committed value. Or performance counters '\Memory\Committed Bytes' and '\Memory\Commit Limit'. Microsoft quote: 'Use this counter to determine whether the pagefile is an appropriate size. Depending on the volatility of your workload, you probably want the pagefile large enough so that it is no more than 50-75 percent used.'

body.tr: Kernel memory dump icin page file >= 800 MB + 5 MB komut bilgisi. Complete memory dump icin >= RAM + 257 MB. Cogu kullanici Automatic memory dump kullanir - Windows kendi yeterli buyukluge ayarlar.
body.en: For kernel memory dump, page file >= 800 MB + 5 MB header. For complete memory dump, >= RAM + 257 MB. Most users use Automatic memory dump - Windows auto-sizes adequately.

title.tr: GUI yolu: Win+R > sysdm.cpl > Advanced > Performance Settings > Advanced > Virtual memory Change
title.en: GUI path: Win+R > sysdm.cpl > Advanced > Performance Settings > Advanced > Virtual memory Change

body.tr: GUI'da 'Automatically manage paging file size for all drives' kutusunun isaretini kaldir. Disk sec > Custom size > Initial size = Maximum size = olculen commit charge'in %150'si. C: SSD ise pagefile orada kalsin (Windows boylesi optimum). HDD'ye tasimak SADECE SSD asindirma endisesi varsa onerilir - ki modern SSD'lerde gereksiz.
body.en: In GUI, uncheck 'Automatically manage paging file size for all drives'. Select drive > Custom size > Initial size = Maximum size = 150% of measured commit charge. Leave pagefile on C: SSD (Windows optimum). Move to HDD ONLY if SSD wear is a concern - unnecessary on modern SSDs.
